text.title: Risk Register Examples
text.summary: Worked examples of threat and opportunity registers for software projects, plus agentic workflow patterns.

example.note: Five scored threats with response strategies — each has a concrete trigger and named owner role.
example.code: | ID | Risk | Cat | P | I | Score | Strategy | Response | Owner | |------|-----------------------------|-------|---|---|-------|----------|----------------------------------|--------| | R-01 | Key developer leaves | Org | M | H | 12 | Mitigate | Document code, cross-train | PM | | R-02 | Third-party API deprecated | Ext | L | VH| 10 | Accept | Abstraction layer; monitor feeds | ArchLd | | R-03 | Scope creep from sponsor | PM | H | M | 12 | Avoid | Change control process enforced | PM | | R-04 | Performance under load | Tech | M | M | 9 | Mitigate | Load testing at sprint 3 | QA Ld | | R-05 | Budget cut mid-project | Org | L | H | 8 | Accept | Identify optional features list | PM |

example.note: Positive risks with Exploit/Enhance strategies; often omitted by default — include at least 1 per 10 threats.
example.code: | ID | Opportunity | P | I | Score | Strategy | Response | |------|-------------------------|---|---|-------|----------|-----------------------------| | O-01 | Early delivery possible | M | H | 12 | Enhance | Fast-track critical path | | O-02 | Partnership offer | L | VH| 10 | Exploit | Prioritise integration work | | O-03 | Positive press coverage | M | M | 9 | Share | Prepare press kit in advance|

section.title: Agentic Two-Phase Workflow
section.p: Phase 1: risk-identifier reads project artefacts (charter, WBS, stakeholder register, recent incidents) and produces candidate risks YAML grouped by PMBoK category. Phase 2: risk-scorer assigns probability x impact with rationale and proposes strategy + response plan. Human review between phases. Weekly: risk-monitor diffs the live register against telemetry (issue tracker, monitoring, schedule) and emits a CHANGES file.

example.note: Structured output schema for risk-scorer; enforces constraints LLMs violate by default.
example.code: { "id": "R-XX", "title": "...", "category": "Technical|External|Organizational|PM", "probability": "VL|L|M|H|VH", "impact": "VL|L|M|H|VH", "score": 12, "strategy": "Avoid|Transfer|Mitigate|Accept|Exploit|Share|Enhance", "response_plan": "imperative sentence", "trigger": "concrete observable signal", "owner": "name or UNASSIGNED", "is_opportunity": false, "contingency_plan": "required if strategy=Accept", "rationale": "2 sentences max" }
